rationale: In the literature positioning, this citation is used to support a core premise: consumers can display behavioral tariff-choice biases under nonlinear pricing. The citing paper then defines its contribution relative to that premise, arguing that such deviations are not only about tariff choice but also about subsequent usage under three-part tariffs. Although the reference appears in a grouped citation, its function is clear in framing and motivating the paper's main extension.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Lambrecht and Skiera (2006) as prior evidence of tariff-choice bias (customers choosing non-cost-minimizing plans) and extends that line by analyzing how tariff structure with free allowances changes post-choice usage and related revenue outcomes. In this sense, it moves from choice bias documentation to a joint choice-usage and learning framework.
